---
title: "Justice Biography Codebook"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Justice Biography Codebook}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

**HCDB Justices Biographical Codebook (justice_bio)**

**Introduction**

This document reflects the coding rules for the High Court Justice
Biographical Database. This codebook was specifically and deliberatively
modelled off the U.S. Supreme Court Justices Database Code Book in order
to facilitate comparative judicial politics research, as well as provide
a robust dataset for Australian researchers interested in engaging with
the empirical study of judicial politics.

This first iteration of the database extends from justices serving on
the Court on January 1, 1986 to those serving on the Court on December
31, 2017.

The unit of analysis is the justice; sometimes referred to as the
nominee in the codebook (terms used interchangeably). Only those
justices who were sworn in as a justice or chief justice of the High
Court are included in the dataset. A handful of justices appear twice by
virtue of being nominated as a puisne justice and later as chief
justice.

Note:

-   Some information might be difficult to locate. If it is impossible
    to locate the information, this is coded 888.

-   Some variables will be inapplicable to a particular justice. Where
    the variable in inapplicable, this is coded 999.

**Contents**

Identification Variables

1\. Justice ID

2\. Justice Name

3\. Date First Nomination Announced

4\. Position to which First Nominated to Court

5\. Date of Swearing In Ceremony

6\. Home Registry

7\. Seat Identification Number

8\. Justice who Nominee Replaced

9\. Chief Justice When Nomination Announced

10\. Chief Justice When Sworn In

11\. Nominating Prime Minister

12\. Nominating Prime Minister Party

13\. Political Power on Date of Nomination

14\. Prime Minister on Date of Swearing In

15\. Prime Minister Party on Date of Swearing In

16\. Political Power on Date of Swearing In Ceremony

17\. Date of Nomination of Sitting Puisne Justice to Chief Justice

18\. Nominating Prime Minister to Chief Justice from Puisne Justice

19\. Nominating Prime Minister Party for Chief Justice from Puisne
Justice

20\. Political Power Variable for Chief Justice from Puisne Justice on
Date of Nomination

21\. Year of Appointment as Chief Justice from Puisne Justice

22\. Date of Swearing In Ceremony as Chief Justice from Puisne Justice

23\. Who Puisne Justice Replaced as Chief Justice

24\. Ideology Score

Personal Background Variables

25\. Year of Birth

26\. Exact Date of Birth

27\. State or Country of Birth

28\. Religion---General

29\. Religion---Specific

30\. Nominee's Race

31\. Nominee's Gender

32\. Year of Death

33\. Exact Date of Death

34\. Age at Date of Death

35\. Year Departed Court

36\. Exact Date of Departure from Court

37\. Age on Date of Departure

38\. Reason for Departing Court

39\. Resignation Party

40\. Prime Minister on Date of Departure

41\. Prime Minister Party on Date of Departure

42\. Political Power on Date Retirement Announced

43\. Position Held by Justice After Departure from Court 1

44\. Party Appointing to Position Held by Justice After Departure from
Court 1

45\. Position Held by Justice After Departure from Court 2

46\. Party Appointing to Position Held by Justice After Departure from
Court 2

47\. Position Held by Justice After Departure from Court 3

48\. Party Appointing to Position Held by Justice After Departure from
Court 3

49\. Position Held by Justice After Departure from Court 4

50\. Party Appointing to Position Held by Justice After Departure from
Court 4

51\. Childhood Location---General (Country or State)

52\. Childhood Location---Specific (City or Town)

53\. Childhood Surrounds

Family Information

54\. Childhood Economic Status

55\. Primary Occupation of Father

56\. Political Affiliation Father

57\. Primary Occupation of Mother

58\. Political Affiliation Mother

59\. Primary Occupation of Spouse

60\. Political Affiliation Spouse

61\. Number of Marriages

62\. Date of Last Marriage

63\. Number of Children

Educational Background Variables

66\. Type of Secondary Education

67\. First Degree

68\. First Degree Institution

69\. First Degree Status

70\. First Degree Year Started

71\. First Degree Year Completed

72\. Second Degree

73\. Second Degree Institution

74\. Second Degree Status

75\. Second Degree Year Started

76\. Second Degree Year Completed

77\. Third Degree

78\. Third Degree Institution

79\. Third Degree Status

80\. Third Degree Year Started

81\. Third Degree Year Completed

82\. Name of Law School

83\. Year of Law School

84\. Academic Honours Medal

85\. Academic Honours First Class Honours

86\. Academic Honours Overseas Scholarship

87\. Overseas Study

88\. Overseas Study Location

89\. First Graduate Degree

90\. First Graduate Degree Institution

91\. First Graduate Degree Status

92\. Second Graduate Degree

93\. Second Graduate Degree Institution

94\. Second Graduate Degree Status

95\. Third Graduate Degree

96\. Third Graduate Degree Institution

97\. Third Graduate Degree Status

98\. Last Year Attended Graduate Institution

Employment Background Variables

99\. Position When Nominated

100\. Position When Nominated Location

101\. Nominated from Judiciary

102\. Prior Judicial Service

103\. Total Months Judicial Service

104\. Number of Months State Court Service

105\. Number of Months Federal Court Service

106\. Number of Months Appellate Court Service

107\. Number of Months Trial Court Service

108\. Prior Judicial Service 1---General

109\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Location

110\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Specific

111\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Start Date

112\. Prior Judicial Service 1---End Date

113\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Months Served

114\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Year First Served

115\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Year Last Served

116\. Prior Judicial Service 1---Party Appointing

117\. Prior Judicial Service 2---General

118\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Location

119\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Specific

120\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Start Date

121\. Prior Judicial Service 2---End Date

122\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Months Served

123\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Year First Served

124\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Year Last Served

125\. Prior Judicial Service 2---Party Appointing

126\. Prior Judicial Service 3---General

127\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Location

128\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Specific

129\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Start Date

130\. Prior Judicial Service 3---End Date

131\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Months Served

132\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Year First Served

133\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Year Last Served

134\. Prior Judicial Service 3---Party Appointing

135\. Admission to the Bar

136\. Year of First Admission to the Bar

137\. State in Which First Admitted to the Bar

138\. Promotion to Senior Counsel

139\. Year Promoted to Senior Counsel

140\. High Court Appearances

141\. Practice as Solicitor

142\. Year First Practiced as Solicitor

143\. Year Last Practiced as Solicitor

144\. State Where First Admitted as Solicitor

145\. Law Teaching

146\. High Court Associateship

147\. Year of High Court Associateship

148\. Justice for High Court Associateship

149\. Other Judicial Associateship

150\. Year of Other Judicial Associateship

151\. Government Appointment Type

152\. Government Appointment Party

153\. Political Party Commonwealth

154\. Known Political Identification

155\. Notes

Identification Variables

1.  Justice ID

Variable Label: justiceID

This variable provides a unique identification number for each of the
justices. This replicates the variable of the same label in the HCDB.
The decision was made to include only justices who were sworn in as
justices of the HCA, rather than including those who were nominated and
not sworn in.

> **Values:**
>
> 01 Griffith02 Barton03 O\'Connor04 Isaacs05 Higgins06 Duffy07 Powers08
> Piddington09 Rich10 Knox11 Starke12 Dixon13 Evatt14 McTiernan15
> Latham16 Williams17 Webb18 Fullagar19 Kitto20 Taylor21 Menzies22
> Windeyer23 Owen24 Barwick25 Walsh26 Gibbs27 Stephen28 Mason29 Jacobs30
> Murphy31 Aickin32 Wilson33 Brennan34 Deane35 Dawson36 Toohey37
> Gaudron38 McHugh39 Gummow40 Kirby41 Hayne42 Callinan43 MGleeson44
> Heydon45 Crennan46 Kiefel47 French48 Bell49 Gageler50 Keane51 Nettle52
> Gordon53 Edelman
>
> 54 Steward
>
> 55 JGleeson

\--*End Content for Variable Justice ID\--*

2.  Justice Name

Variable Label: justiceName

This variable provides a unique name for each justice using a last name
convention with first initials for disambiguation. See variable Justice
ID for the full list of names.

\--*End Content for Variable Justice Name\--*

3.  Date First Nomination Announced

Variable Label: dateNom

This variable contains the year, month, and day that the first
nomination of the justice was announced. A justice will only have two
nominations if she/he was first nominated as a puisne judge and
subsequently as chief justice. Coders should hard code the date in a
8-digit format as YEAR MONTH DAY. For example, if a judge's first
nomination to the Court was announced on 7 May 2015, it will be recorded
as 20150507.

\--*End Content for Variable Date First Nomination Announced\--*

4.  Position to which First Nominated to Court

Variable Label: position

This variable captures the position for which the nominee was first
nominated to the Court.

Values:

> 1 Chief Justice
>
> 2 Puisne Justice

*\--End Content for Variable Position to which First Nominated to
Court\--*

5.  Date of Swearing In Ceremony

Variable Label: dateSwearingIn

This variable identifies the exact date the judge was sworn in to
his/her position on the Court. The information is below.

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Nominee Code  Nominee                           Date of Swearing In
  ------------- --------------------------------- ------------------------
  01            Sir Samuel Griffith               05101903

  02            Sir Edmund Barton                 05101903

  03            Richard O\'Connor                 05101903

  04            Sir Isaac Isaacs                  12101903

  05            H. B. Higgins                     13101903

  06            Sir Frank Gavan Duffy             11021913

  07            Sir Charles Powers                05031913

  08            Albert Piddington                 06031913

  09            Sir George Rich                   05041913

  10            Sir Adrian Knox                   18101919

  11            Sir Hayden Starke                 05021920

  12            Sir Owen Dixon                    04021929

  13            H. V. Evatt                       19121930

  14            Sir Edward McTiernan              20121930

  15            Sir John Latham                   11101935

  16            Sir Dudley Williams               15101940

  17            Sir William Webb                  16051946

  18            Sir Wilfred Fullagar              08021950

  19            Sir Frank Kitto                   10051950

  20            Sir Alan Taylor                   03091952

  21            Sir Douglas Menzies               12061958

  22            Sir Victor Windeyer               08091958

  23            Sir William Owen                  22091961

  24            Sir Garfield Barwick              27041964

  25            Sir Cyril Walsh                   20091969

  26            Sir Harry Gibbs                   04081970

  27            Sir Ninian Stephen                01031972

  28            Sir Anthony Mason                 07081972

  29            Sir Kenneth Jacobs                08021974

  30            Lionel Murphy                     10021975

  31            Sir Keith Aickin                  20091976

  32            Sir Ronald Wilson                 21051979

  33            Sir Gerard Brennan                12021981

  34            Sir William Deane                 25061982

  35            Sir Daryl Dawson                  30071982

  36            John Toohey                       06021987

  37            Mary Gaudron                      06021987

  38            Michael McHugh                    14021989

  39            William Gummow                    21041995

  40            Michael Kirby                     06021996

  41            Kenneth Hayne                     22091997

  42            Ian Callinan                      03021998

  43            Murray Gleeson                    22051998

  44            Dyson Heydon                      01022003

  45            Susan Crennan                     01112005

  46            Susan Kiefel                      04092007

  47            Robert French                     01092008

  48            Virginia Bell                     03022009

  49            Stephen Gageler                   09102012

  50            Patrick Keane                     05032013

  51            Geoffrey Nettle                   03022015

  52            Michelle Gordon                   09062015

  53            James Edelman                     30012017
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------

\--*End Content for Variable Date of Swearing In Ceremony\--*

6.  Home Registry

Variable Label: homeRegistry

Variable denotes the registry where the justice works/sits when not in
Canberra.

**Values**:

> 1 Adelaide
>
> 2 Brisbane
>
> 3 Canberra
>
> 4 Darwin
>
> 5 Hobart
>
> 6 Melbourne
>
> 7 Perth
>
> 8 Sydney

\--*End Content for Variable Home Registry\--*

7.  Seat Identification Number

Variable Label: SeatID

Seat number refers to the number of seats on the Court and who has
occupied the seat at any given time. Wikipedia (I know...) has this
information in graphic form:
<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Justices_of_the_High_Court_of_Australia>

**Values**:

> 01 First Seat
>
> 02 Second Seat
>
> 03 Third Seat
>
> 04 Fourth Seat
>
> 05 Fifth Seat
>
> 06 Sixth Seat
>
> 07 Seventh Seat

\--*End Content for Variable Seat Identification Number\--*

8.  Justice who Nominee Replaced

Variable Label: justiceReplaced

This variable identifies the justice whom the nominee replaced.

**Values:**

> 01 Griffith02 Barton03 O\'Connor04 Isaacs05 Higgins06 Duffy07 Powers08
> Piddington09 Rich10 Knox11 Starke12 Dixon13 Evatt14 McTiernan15
> Latham16 Williams17 Webb18 Fullagar19 Kitto20 Taylor21 Menzies22
> Windeyer23 Owen24 Barwick25 Walsh26 Gibbs27 Stephen28 Mason29 Jacobs30
> Murphy31 Aickin32 Wilson33 Brennan34 Deane35 Dawson36 Toohey37
> Gaudron38 McHugh39 Gummow40 Kirby41 Hayne42 Callinan43 Gleeson44
> Heydon45 Crennan46 Kiefel47 French48 Bell49 Gageler50 Keane51 Nettle52
> Gordon53 Edelman

\--*End Content for Variable Justice ID Who Nominee Replaced\--*

9.  Chief Justice When Nomination Announced

Variable Label: chiefAnnounce

This variable identifies the Chief Justice of the Court when the nominee
was appointed, where appointment is defined as the date of the swearing
in ceremony. The Chief Justice is to be identified by their Chief
Justice ID specified below, not their Justice ID.

Values:

> 1 Griffith
>
> 2 Knox
>
> 3 Isaacs
>
> 4 Duffy
>
> 5 Latham
>
> 6 Dixon
>
> 7 Barwick
>
> 8 Gibbs
>
> 9 Mason
>
> 10 Brennan
>
> 11 Gleeson
>
> 12 French
>
> 13 Kiefel

\--*End Content for Variable Chief Justice When Nomination Announced\--*

10. Chief Justice When Sworn In

Variable Label: chiefSworn

This variable identifies the Chief Justice of the Court when the nominee
was sworn in. Where the nominee is to the position of Chief Justice,
coders should code the nominee by Chief Justice ID (below). Where the
nominee is sworn in alongside a new Chief Justice (e.g. Edelman with
Keifel), the code for the new Chief Justice should be entered.

Values:

> 1 Griffith
>
> 2 Knox
>
> 3 Isaacs
>
> 4 Duffy
>
> 5 Latham
>
> 6 Dixon
>
> 7 Barwick
>
> 8 Gibbs
>
> 9 Mason
>
> 10 Brennan
>
> 11 Gleeson
>
> 12 French
>
> 13 Kiefel

\--*End Content for Variable Chief Justice When Sworn In\--*

11. Nominating Prime Minister

Variable Label: nomPM

This variable indicates the Prime Minister on the date the nominee was
announced.

**Values:**

> 1 Barton (01.01.1901 -- 24.09.1903)
>
> 2 Deakin1 (24.09.1903 -- 27.04.1904)
>
> 3 Watson (27.04.1904 -- 17.08.1904)
>
> 4 Reid (18.08.1904 -- 05.07.1905)
>
> 5 Deakin 2 (05.07.1905 -- 13.11.1908)
>
> 6 Fisher 1 (13.11.1908 -- 02.06.19090
>
> 7 Deakin 3 (02.06.1909 -- 29.04.1910)
>
> 8 Fisher 2 (29.04.1910 -- 24.06.1913)
>
> 9 Cook (24.06.1913 -- 17.09.1914)
>
> 10 Fisher 3 (17.09.1914 -- 27.10.1915)
>
> 11 Hughes (27.10.1915 -- 09.02.1923)
>
> 12 Bruce (09.02.1923 -- 22.10.1929)
>
> 13 Scullin (22.10.1929 -- 06.01.1932)
>
> 14 Lyons (06.01.1932 -- 07.04.1939)
>
> 15 Page (07.04.1939 -- 26.04.1939)
>
> 16 Menzies 1 (26.04.1939 -- 29.08.194)
>
> 17 Fadden (29.08.1941 -- 07.10.1941)
>
> 18 Curtin (07.10.1941 -- 05.07.1945)
>
> 19 Forde (06.07.1945 -- 13.07.1945)
>
> 20 Chifley (13.07.1945 -- 19.12.1949)
>
> 21 Menzies 2 (19.12.1949 -- 26.01.1966)
>
> 22 Holt (26.01.1966 -- 19.12.1967)
>
> 23 McEwen (19.12.1967 -- 10.01.1968)
>
> 24 Gorton (10.01.1968 -- 10.03.1971)
>
> 25 McMahon(10.03.1971 -- 05.12.1972)
>
> 26 Whitlam (05.12.1972 -- 11.11.1975)
>
> 27 Fraser (11.11.1975 -- 11.03.1983)
>
> 28 Hawke (11.03.1983 -- 20.12.1991)
>
> 29 Keating (20.12.1991 -- 11.03.1996)
>
> 30 Howard (11.03.1996 -- 3.12.2007)
>
> 31 Rudd 1 (03.12.2007 -- 24.06.2010)
>
> 32 Gillard (24.06.2010 -- 27.06.2013)
>
> 33 Rudd 2 (27.06.2013 -- 18.09.2013)
>
> 34 Abbott (18.09.2013 -- 15.09.2015)
>
> 35 Turnbull (15.09.2015 -- present)
>
> 36 Morrison

\--*End Content for Variable Nominating Prime Minister\--*

12. Nominating Prime Minister Party

Variable Label: nomPMParty

This variable identifies the political party of the Prime Minister on
the date the nomination was announced.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

3 Country

4 United Australia

5 Nationalist

6 Protectionist

7 Commonwealth Liberal

8 Free Trade

\--*End Content for Variable Nominating Prime Minister Party\--*

13. Political Power on Date of Nomination

Variable Label: politicalPowerDateNom

This variable indicates the controlling political party in both the
House of Representatives and the Senate on the date the nomination was
announced.

**Values:**

1 Coalition House/Coalition Senate

2 Coalition House/Not Coalition Senate

3 Labour House/Labour Senate

4 Labour House/Not Labour Senate

5 Neither/Neither

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------
                             **Party in Control**  
  -------------------------- --------------------- -----------------------
  **Election**               **House**             **Senate**

                                                   

  Dec 1, 1984                Labour                Not Labour

  July 11, 1987              Labour                Not Labour

  Mar 24, 1990               Labour                Not Labour

  Mar 13, 1993               Labour                Not Labour

  Oct 3, 1998                Coalition             Not Coalition

  Nov 10, 2001               Coalition             Not Coalition

  Oct 9, 2004                Coalition             Coalition

  Nov 24, 2007               Labour                Not Labour

  Aug 21, 2010               Neither               Neither

  Sept 7, 2013               Coalition             Neither

  July 2, 2016               Coalition             Neither
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------

\--

*End Content for Variable Political Power on Date of Nomination---*

14. Prime Minister on Date of Swearing In

Variable Label: PMSwearingIn

This variable indicates the Prime Minister on the date the nominee was
sworn in. It is likely this is the same as the Prime Minister on the
date the nomination was announced, but this variable is included just in
case.

**Values:**

> 1 Barton (01.01.1901 -- 24.09.1903)
>
> 2 Deakin1 (24.09.1903 -- 27.04.1904)
>
> 3 Watson (27.04.1904 -- 17.08.1904)
>
> 4 Reid (18.08.1904 -- 05.07.1905)
>
> 5 Deakin 2 (05.07.1905 -- 13.11.1908)
>
> 6 Fisher 1 (13.11.1908 -- 02.06.19090
>
> 7 Deakin 3 (02.06.1909 -- 29.04.1910)
>
> 8 Fisher 2 (29.04.1910 -- 24.06.1913)
>
> 9 Cook (24.06.1913 -- 17.09.1914)
>
> 10 Fisher 3 (17.09.1914 -- 27.10.1915)
>
> 11 Hughes (27.10.1915 -- 09.02.1923)
>
> 12 Bruce (09.02.1923 -- 22.10.1929)
>
> 13 Scullin (22.10.1929 -- 06.01.1932)
>
> 14 Lyons (06.01.1932 -- 07.04.1939)
>
> 15 Page (07.04.1939 -- 26.04.1939)
>
> 16 Menzies 1 (26.04.1939 -- 29.08.194)
>
> 17 Fadden (29.08.1941 -- 07.10.1941)
>
> 18 Curtin (07.10.1941 -- 05.07.1945)
>
> 19 Forde (06.07.1945 -- 13.07.1945)
>
> 20 Chifley (13.07.1945 -- 19.12.1949)
>
> 21 Menzies 2 (19.12.1949 -- 26.01.1966)
>
> 22 Holt (26.01.1966 -- 19.12.1967)
>
> 23 McEwen (19.12.1967 -- 10.01.1968)
>
> 24 Gorton (10.01.1968 -- 10.03.1971)
>
> 25 McMahon(10.03.1971 -- 05.12.1972)
>
> 26 Whitlam (05.12.1972 -- 11.11.1975)
>
> 27 Fraser (11.11.1975 -- 11.03.1983)
>
> 28 Hawke (11.03.1983 -- 20.12.1991)
>
> 29 Keating (20.12.1991 -- 11.03.1996)
>
> 30 Howard (11.03.1996 -- 3.12.2007)
>
> 31 Rudd 1 (03.12.2007 -- 24.06.2010)
>
> 32 Gillard (24.06.2010 -- 27.06.2013)
>
> 33 Rudd 2 (27.06.2013 -- 18.09.2013)
>
> 34 Abbott (18.09.2013 -- 15.09.2015)
>
> 35 Turnbull (15.09.2015 -- present)

\--*End Content for Variable Prime Minister on Date of Swearing In\--*

15. Prime Minister Party on Date of Swearing
    In

Variable Label: PMPartySwearingIn

This variable indicates the Prime Minister's party on the date the
nominee was sworn in. It is likely this is the same as the Prime
Minister on the date the nomination was announced, but this variable is
included just in case.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

3 Country

4 United Australia

5 Nationalist

6 Protectionist

7 Commonwealth Liberal

8 Free Trade

\--*End Content for Variable Prime Minister Party on Date of Swearing
In\--*

16. Political Power on Date of Swearing In
    Ceremony

Variable Label: politicalPowerSwearingIn

This variable indicates the controlling political party in both the
House of Representatives and the Senate on the date of the High Court
decision. It is likely this is the same as on the date the nomination
was announced, but this variable is included just in case.

Values:

1 Coalition House/Coalition Senate

2 Coalition House/Not Coalition Senate

3 Labour House/Labour Senate

4 Labour House/Not Labour Senate

5 Neither/Neither

+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
|                         | > **Party in        |                      |
|                         | > Control**         |                      |
+=========================+=====================+======================+
| > **Election**          | > **House**         | > **Senate**         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
|                         |                     |                      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Dec 1, 1984           | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > July 11, 1987         | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Mar 24, 1990          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Mar 13, 1993          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Oct 3, 1998           | > Coalition         | > Not Coalition      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Nov 10, 2001          | > Coalition         | > Not Coalition      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Oct 9, 2004           | > Coalition         | > Coalition          |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Nov 24, 2007          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Aug 21, 2010          | > Neither           | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Sept 7, 2013          | > Coalition         | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > July 2, 2016          | > Coalition         | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+

\--*End Content for Variable Political Power on Date of Swearing In
Ceremony\--*

17. Date of Nomination of Sitting Puisne
    Justice to Chief Justice

Variable Label: promotionDate

This variable notes the date that a sitting puisne justice was nominated
(announcement date) to the position of Chief Justice. This variable is
designed to capture information about a second nomination of the same
justice and will be applicable in a limited number of cases.

\--*End Content for Variable Date of Nomination of Sitting Puisne
Justice to Chief Justice\--*

18. Nominating Prime Minister to Chief Justice
    from Puisne Justice

Variable Label: nomPMPromotion

This variable indicates the Prime Minister on the date of the nomination
from puisne justice to Chief Justice. This variable is designed to
capture information about a second nomination of the same justice and
will be applicable in a limited number of cases.

**Values:**

1 Barton (01.01.1901 -- 24.09.1903)

> 2 Deakin1 (24.09.1903 -- 27.04.1904)
>
> 3 Watson (27.04.1904 -- 17.08.1904)
>
> 4 Reid (18.08.1904 -- 05.07.1905)
>
> 5 Deakin 2 (05.07.1905 -- 13.11.1908)
>
> 6 Fisher 1 (13.11.1908 -- 02.06.19090
>
> 7 Deakin 3 (02.06.1909 -- 29.04.1910)
>
> 8 Fisher 2 (29.04.1910 -- 24.06.1913)
>
> 9 Cook (24.06.1913 -- 17.09.1914)
>
> 10 Fisher 3 (17.09.1914 -- 27.10.1915)
>
> 11 Hughes (27.10.1915 -- 09.02.1923)
>
> 12 Bruce (09.02.1923 -- 22.10.1929)
>
> 13 Scullin (22.10.1929 -- 06.01.1932)
>
> 14 Lyons (06.01.1932 -- 07.04.1939)
>
> 15 Page (07.04.1939 -- 26.04.1939)
>
> 16 Menzies 1 (26.04.1939 -- 29.08.194)
>
> 17 Fadden (29.08.1941 -- 07.10.1941)
>
> 18 Curtin (07.10.1941 -- 05.07.1945)
>
> 19 Forde (06.07.1945 -- 13.07.1945)
>
> 20 Chifley (13.07.1945 -- 19.12.1949)
>
> 21 Menzies 2 (19.12.1949 -- 26.01.1966)
>
> 22 Holt (26.01.1966 -- 19.12.1967)
>
> 23 McEwen (19.12.1967 -- 10.01.1968)
>
> 24 Gorton (10.01.1968 -- 10.03.1971)
>
> 25 McMahon(10.03.1971 -- 05.12.1972)
>
> 26 Whitlam (05.12.1972 -- 11.11.1975)
>
> 27 Fraser (11.11.1975 -- 11.03.1983)
>
> 28 Hawke (11.03.1983 -- 20.12.1991)
>
> 29 Keating (20.12.1991 -- 11.03.1996)
>
> 30 Howard (11.03.1996 -- 3.12.2007)
>
> 31 Rudd 1 (03.12.2007 -- 24.06.2010)
>
> 32 Gillard (24.06.2010 -- 27.06.2013)
>
> 33 Rudd 2 (27.06.2013 -- 18.09.2013)
>
> 34 Abbott (18.09.2013 -- 15.09.2015)
>
> 35 Turnbull (15.09.2015 -- present)

\--*End Content for Variable Nominating Prime Minister to Chief Justice
from Puisne Justice\--*

19. Nominating Prime Minister Party for Chief
    Justice from Puisne Justice

Variable Label: nomPMPartyPromotion

This variable is designed to capture information about a second
nomination of the same justice and will be applicable in a limited
number of cases.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

3 Country

4 United Australia

5 Nationalist

6 Protectionist

7 Commonwealth Liberal

8 Free Trade

*\--End Content for Variable Nominating Prime Minister Party for Chief
Justice from Puisne Justice \--*

20. Political Power Variable for Chief Justice
    from Puisne Justice on Date of Nomination

Variable Label: politicalPowerPromotion

This variable indicates the controlling political party in both the
House of Representatives and the Senate on the date of the nomination.
This variable is designed to capture information about a second
nomination of the same justice and will be applicable in a limited
number of cases.

**Values:**

1 Coalition House/Coalition Senate

2 Coalition House/Not Coalition Senate

3 Labour House/Labour Senate

4 Labour House/Not Labour Senate

5 Neither/Neither

+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
|                         | > **Party in        |                      |
|                         | > Control**         |                      |
+=========================+=====================+======================+
| > **Election**          | > **House**         | > **Senate**         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Dec 1, 1984           | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > July 11, 1987         | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Mar 24, 1990          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Mar 13, 1993          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Oct 3, 1998           | > Coalition         | > Not Coalition      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Nov 10, 2001          | > Coalition         | > Not Coalition      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Oct 9, 2004           | > Coalition         | > Coalition          |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Nov 24, 2007          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Aug 21, 2010          | > Neither           | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Sept 7, 2013          | > Coalition         | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > July 2, 2016          | > Coalition         | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+

\--*End Content for Variable Political Power for Chief Justice from
Puisne Justice on Date of Nomination\--*

21. Year of Appointment as Chief Justice from
    Puisne Justice

Variable Label: yearPromotion

Enter year the nominee was promoted to chief justice.

\--*End Content for Variable Year of Appointment as Chief Justice\--*

22. Date of Swearing In Ceremony as Chief
    Justice from Puisne Justice

Variable Label: dateSwearingInPromotion

This variable identifies the exact date the judge was sworn in to the
position of chief justice.

\--*End Content for Variable Date of Swearing In Ceremony as Chief
Justice\--*

23. Who Puisne Justice Replaced as Chief
    Justice

Variable Label: replacementCJPromotion

This variable identifies who the nominee for Chief Justice position
replaced as Chief Justice. The Chief Justice whom the nominee replaced
is to be identified by their Chief Justice ID specified below.

Values:

> 1 Griffith
>
> 2 Knox
>
> 3 Isaacs
>
> 4 Duffy
>
> 5 Latham
>
> 6 Dixon
>
> 7 Barwick
>
> 8 Gibbs
>
> 9 Mason
>
> 10 Brennan
>
> 11 Gleeson
>
> 12 French
>
> 13 Kiefel

*\--End Content for Variable who Puisne Justice Replaced as Chief
Justice\--*

24. Ideology Score

Variable Label: ideologyScore

\--*End Content for Variable Ideology Score\--*

Personal Background Variables

25. Year of Birth

Variable Label: nomYearBirth

Enter the justice's year of birth.

\--*End Content for Variable Year of Birth\--*

26. Exact Date of Birth

Variable Label: nomDateBirth

This variable contains the justice's date of birth. Coders should hard
code the date in a 8-digit format. For example, if a judge was born on 7
May 2015, it will be recorded as 07052015.

\--*End Content for Variable Exact Date of Birth\--*

27. State or Country of Birth

Variable Label: placeBirthGen

This variable identifies the state of birth for Australian born
justices, or the country of birth for justices born overseas. Codes are
available for all states. If the justice is not born in Australia, hard
code the name of the country and the coding will be modified later.

Values:

> 1 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 2 New South Wales
>
> 3 Northern Territory
>
> 4 Queensland
>
> 5 South Australia
>
> 6 Tasmania
>
> 7 Victoria
>
> 8 Western Australia
>
> 9 Overseas, Canada
>
> 10 Overseas, UK
>
> 11 Overseas, USA

\--*End Content for Variable State or Country of Birth\--*

28. Religion---General

Variable Label: religionGeneral

This variable captures whether the justice identifies as Christian or
not.

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable Religion---General\--*

29. Religion---Specific

Variable Label: religionSpecific

Coders should code the religion of the justice with as much specificity
as possible. Classifications were drawn from the ABS codes for religion.

Values:

+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 1 Christian---Anglican                                              |
| >                                                                     |
| > 2 Christian---Baptist                                               |
+=======================================================================+
| > 3 Christian---Brethren                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 4 Christian---Catholic                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 5 Christian---Churches of Christ                                    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 6 Christian---Jehovah's Witnesses                                   |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 7 Christian---Latter-day Saints                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 8 Christian---Lutheran                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 9 Christian---Oriental Orthodox                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 10 Christian---Assyrian Apostolic                                   |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 11 Christian---Eastern Orthodox                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 12 Christian---Presbyterian and Reformed                            |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 13 Christian---Salvation Army                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 14 Christian---Seventh-day Adventist                                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 15 Christian---Uniting Church                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 16 Christian---Pentecostal                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 17 Other Christian                                                  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 18 Hinduism                                                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 19 Islam                                                            |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 20 Judaism                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 21 Australian Aboriginal Traditional Religions                      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 22 Baha\'i                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 23 Chinese Religions                                                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 24 Druse                                                            |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 25 Japanese Religions                                               |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 26 Nature Religions                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 27 Sikhism                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 28 Spiritualism                                                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 29 Miscellaneous Religions                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 30 No Religion, so described                                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 31 Secular Beliefs                                                  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| > 32 Other Spiritual Beliefs                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

\--*End Content for Variable Religion---Specific\--*

30. Nominee's Race

Variable Label: race

Values:

1 White

2 Asian

3 Indigenous

4 Black

\--*End Content for Variable Race\--*

31. Nominee's Gender

Variable Label: gender

Values:

> 1 Female
>
> 2 Male

\--*End Content for Variable Gender\--*

32. Year of Death

Variable Label: yearDeath

Enter year of death of the justice.

\--*End Content for Variable Year of Death\--*

33. Exact Date of Death

Variable Label: dateDeath

Enter exact date of death in an 8-digit format (e.g. 01012018).

\--*End Content for Variable Exact Date of Death\--*

34. Age at Date of Death

Variable Label: ageDeath

Enter the justices age on his or her date of death.

\--*End Content for Variable Age at Date of Death\--*

35. Year Departed Court

Variable Label: yearDeparture

Enter the year the justice departed the Court.

\--*End Content for Variable Year Departed Court\--*

36. Exact Date of Departure from Court

Variable Label: dateDeparture

Enter exact date of departure from the Court in an 8-digit format (e.g.
01012018).

\--*End Content for Variable Exact Date of Departure from Court\--*

37. Age on Date of Departure

Variable Label: ageDateDeparture

Enter age on date justice departed the Court.

\--*End Content for Variable Age on Date of Departure\--*

38. Reason for Departing Court

Variable Label: reasonDeparture

Values:

1 Died in office

2 Reached constitutional retirement age

> 3 Retired \[defined as no longer participating in the workforce after
> stepping down\]

4 Resigned \[continued to work after stepping down\]

\--*End Content for Variable Reason for Departure\--*

39. Resignation Party

Variable Label: resignationParty

This variable indicates the controlling political party when a justice
resigned, therefore a value is only entered where the reason for
departure variable is coded as 4. This variable is only coded when the
justice resigned earlier than the mandated retirement age. If a justice
retired when constitutionally required then no assumptions can be made
about partisan gaming. However if retirement occurred prior to mandated
retirement age then it is possible that the resignation was timed for
partisan impact. This variable captures this possibility.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Resignation Party\--*

40. Prime Minister on Date of Departure

Variable Label: PMDeparture

This variable indicates the Prime Minister on the date of the justice's
departure from the Court.

**Values:**

1 Barton (01.01.1901 -- 24.09.1903)

> 2 Deakin1 (24.09.1903 -- 27.04.1904)
>
> 3 Watson (27.04.1904 -- 17.08.1904)
>
> 4 Reid (18.08.1904 -- 05.07.1905)
>
> 5 Deakin 2 (05.07.1905 -- 13.11.1908)
>
> 6 Fisher 1 (13.11.1908 -- 02.06.19090
>
> 7 Deakin 3 (02.06.1909 -- 29.04.1910)
>
> 8 Fisher 2 (29.04.1910 -- 24.06.1913)
>
> 9 Cook (24.06.1913 -- 17.09.1914)
>
> 10 Fisher 3 (17.09.1914 -- 27.10.1915)
>
> 11 Hughes (27.10.1915 -- 09.02.1923)
>
> 12 Bruce (09.02.1923 -- 22.10.1929)
>
> 13 Scullin (22.10.1929 -- 06.01.1932)
>
> 14 Lyons (06.01.1932 -- 07.04.1939)
>
> 15 Page (07.04.1939 -- 26.04.1939)
>
> 16 Menzies 1 (26.04.1939 -- 29.08.194)
>
> 17 Fadden (29.08.1941 -- 07.10.1941)
>
> 18 Curtin (07.10.1941 -- 05.07.1945)
>
> 19 Forde (06.07.1945 -- 13.07.1945)
>
> 20 Chifley (13.07.1945 -- 19.12.1949)
>
> 21 Menzies 2 (19.12.1949 -- 26.01.1966)
>
> 22 Holt (26.01.1966 -- 19.12.1967)
>
> 23 McEwen (19.12.1967 -- 10.01.1968)
>
> 24 Gorton (10.01.1968 -- 10.03.1971)
>
> 25 McMahon(10.03.1971 -- 05.12.1972)
>
> 26 Whitlam (05.12.1972 -- 11.11.1975)
>
> 27 Fraser (11.11.1975 -- 11.03.1983)
>
> 28 Hawke (11.03.1983 -- 20.12.1991)
>
> 29 Keating (20.12.1991 -- 11.03.1996)
>
> 30 Howard (11.03.1996 -- 3.12.2007)
>
> 31 Rudd 1 (03.12.2007 -- 24.06.2010)
>
> 32 Gillard (24.06.2010 -- 27.06.2013)
>
> 33 Rudd 2 (27.06.2013 -- 18.09.2013)
>
> 34 Abbott (18.09.2013 -- 15.09.2015)
>
> 35 Turnbull (15.09.2015 -- present)

\--*End Content for Variable Prime Minister on Date of Departure\--*

41. Prime Minister Party on Date of Departure

Variable Label: PMPartyDeparture

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

3 Country

4 United Australia

5 Nationalist

6 Protectionist

7 Commonwealth Liberal

8 Free Trade

\--*End Content for Variable Prime Minister Party on Date of
Departure\--*

42. Political Power on Date Retirement
    Announced

Variable Label: politicalPowerRetirementAnnounce

This variable indicates the controlling political party in both the
House of Representatives and the Senate on the date of the High Court
decision. The normalizations contain both the coding options as well as
the necessary information for political power in any given year.

**Values:**

1 Coalition House/Coalition Senate

2 Coalition House/Not Coalition Senate

3 Labour House/Labour Senate

4 Labour House/Not Labour Senate

5 Neither/Neither

+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
|                         | > **Party in        |                      |
|                         | > Control**         |                      |
+=========================+=====================+======================+
| > **Election**          | > **House**         | > **Senate**         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Dec 1, 1984           | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > July 11, 1987         | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Mar 24, 1990          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Mar 13, 1993          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Oct 3, 1998           | > Coalition         | > Not Coalition      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
|                         |                     |                      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Nov 10, 2001          | > Coalition         | > Not Coalition      |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Oct 9, 2004           | > Coalition         | > Coalition          |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Nov 24, 2007          | > Labour            | > Not Labour         |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Aug 21, 2010          | > Neither           | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > Sept 7, 2013          | > Coalition         | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+
| > July 2, 2016          | > Coalition         | > Neither            |
+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+

\--*End Content for Variable Political Power on Date Retirement
Announced\--*

43. Position Held by Justice After Departure
    from Court 1

Variable Label: positionAfterDeparture1

Enter the role the justice took immediately after departing the Court.

1 Barrister

2 Academic (honorary)

3 Academic (faculty)

4 Academic (administrative, e.g., VC)

5 Foreign judicial service

6 International judicial service

7 Commissioner (Cth)

8 Commissioner (state)

9 Director/chair of foundation or similar organization

10 Governor-General

11 Other government service (Cth)

12 Other government service (state)

\--*End Content for Variable Position Held by Justice After Departure
from Court 1\--*

44. Party Appointing to Position Held by
    Justice After Departure from Court 1

Variable Label: positionAfterDepartureParty1

1 Liberal

2 Labor

\--*End Content for Variable Party Appointing Position Held by Justice
After Departure from Court 1\--*

45. Position Held by Justice After Departure
    from Court 2

Variable Label: positionAfterDeparture2

This variable facilitates the capture of situations where the justice
held multiple roles after departing the Court. If there are more than
two, enter them here and make a note in the comments.

\--*End Content for Variable Position Held by Justice After Departure
from Court 2\--*

46. Party Appointing to Position Held by
    Justice After Departure from Court 2

Variable Label: positionAfterDepartureParty2

If appointment by government was required for the second role the
justice took after departing the Court, enter the name of the party
appointing the justice to the role (e.g. Labour).

\--*End Content for Variable Party Appointing Position Held by Justice
After Departure from Court 2\--*

47. Position Held by Justice After Departure
    from Court 3

Variable Label: positionAfterDeparture3

This variable facilitates the capture of situations where the justice
held multiple roles after departing the Court. If there are more than
two, enter them here and make a note in the comments.

\--*End Content for Variable Position Held by Justice After Departure
from Court 3\--*

48. Party Appointing to Position Held by
    Justice After Departure from Court 3

Variable Label: positionAfterDepartureParty3

If appointment by government was required for the third role the justice
took after departing the Court, enter the name of the party appointing
the justice to the role (e.g. Labour).

\--*End Content for Variable Party Appointing Position Held by Justice
After Departure from Court 3\--*

49. Position Held by Justice After Departure
    from Court 4

Variable Label: positionAfterDeparture4

This variable facilitates the capture of situations where the justice
held multiple roles after departing the Court. If there are more than
two, enter them here and make a note in the comments.

\--*End Content for Variable Position Held by Justice After Departure
from Court 4\--*

50. Party Appointing to Position Held by
    Justice After Departure from Court 4

Variable Label: positionAfterDepartureParty4

If appointment by government was required for the third role the justice
took after departing the Court, enter the name of the party appointing
the justice to the role (e.g. Labour).

\--*End Content for Variable Party Appointing Position Held by Justice
After Departure from Court 4\--*

51. Childhood Location---General (Country or
    State)

Variable Label: childhoodLocationGeneral

Variable identifies the general childhood location of the justice. If
the childhood location is not captured below, enter the location.

Values:

> 1 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 2 New South Wales
>
> 3 Northern Territory
>
> 4 Queensland
>
> 5 South Australia
>
> 6 Tasmania
>
> 7 Victoria
>
> 8 Western Australia
>
> 9 UK
>
> 10 USA

\--*End Content for Variable Childhood Location---General\--*

52. Childhood Location---Specific (City or
    Town)

Variable Label: childhoodLocationSpecific

Enter the specific city or town where the justice spent the majority of
their childhood.

1 Capital city

2 Town

\--*End Content for Variable Childhood Location---Specific\--*

53. Childhood Surrounds

Variable Label: childhoodSurrounds

Variable refers to the general environment in which the justice spent
their childhood. Where the family moved, the code should reflect the
most prominent experience.

Values:

> 1 Family farm
>
> 2 Rural
>
> 3 Small town
>
> 4 Small city
>
> 5 Urban (large/larger city)

\--*End Content for Variable Childhood Surrounds\--*

Family Information

54. Childhood Economic Status

Variable Label: economicStatus

This variable indicates the general socioeconomic status of the
justice's family during his or her childhood. Where the family suffered
upward or downward shifts in economic status the coder should code the
variable according to that status that describes the largest segment of
the justice's childhood.

Values:

1 Lower

2 Lower-middle

3 Middle

4 Upper-middle

5 Upper

\--*End Content for Variable Childhood Economic Status\--*

55. Primary Occupation of Father

Variable Label: occupationFather

Enter father's occupation.

1 Homemaker

2 Politics

3 Public service

4 Professional, employee

5 Military

6 Professional, self-employed

7 Blue collar

8 Lawyer

9 Academic

10 Teacher

11 Administrative role

\--*End Content for Variable Primary Occupation of Father\--*

56. Political Affiliation Father

Variable Label: politicalFather

Variable denotes whether the justice's father was formally associated
with a political party and worked in a political role for that party.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Political Affiliation Father\--*

57. Primary Occupation of Mother

Variable Label: occupationMother

Enter mother's occupation.

\--*End Content for Variable Primary Occupation of Mother\--*

58. Political Affiliation Mother

Variable Label: politicalMother

Variable denotes whether the justice's mother was formally associated
with a political party and worked in a political role for that party.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Political Affiliation Mother\--*

59. Primary Occupation of Spouse

Variable Label: occupationSpouse

Enter spouse's occupation.

\--*End Content for Variable Primary Occupation of Spouse\--*

60. Political Affiliation Spouse

Variable Label: politicalSpouse

Variable denotes whether the justice's spouse was formally associated
with a political party and worked in a political role for that party.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Political Affiliation Spouse\--*

61. Number of Marriages

Variable Label: numberMarriages

Enter the number of marriages of the justice. Include same sex domestic
partnerships prior to the legalization of same sex marriage.

\--*End Content for Variable Number Marriages\--*

62. Date of Last Marriage

Variable Label: dateMarraige

Enter the date of the justices last marriage.

\--*End Content for Variable Date of Last Marriage\--*

63. Number of Children

Variable Label: numberChildren

Enter the number of children the justice has.

\--*End Content for Variable Number of Children\--*

64. **Number of Daughters**

Variable Label: numberDaughters

Enter the number of daughters the justice has.

\--*End Content for Variable Number of Daughters\--*

65. **Number of Sons**

Variable Label: numberSons

Enter the number of sons the justice has.

\--*End Content for Variable Number of Sons\--*

Educational Background Variables

66. Type of Secondary Education

Variable Label: secondaryEducationType

Values:

1 Public

2 Private---coeducational

3 Private---single sex

\--*End Content for Variable Type of Secondary Education\--*

67. First Degree

Variable Label: firstDegree

This variable reflects the first degree commenced by the justice. Enter
degree name (e.g. BA) and major if the information is available (e.g.
history). Where the justice undertook a double degree, disaggregate the
degrees and list one under first degree and the other under second
degree. Where a justice commenced by did not finish (e.g., because of
transferring the degree program), the degree should still be included.

1 Bachelor of Laws

2 Bachelor of Arts

3 Bachelor of Economics

4 Bachelor of Commerce

5 Bachelor of Jurisprudence

6 Bachelor of Science

\--*End Content for Variable First Degree\--*

68. First Degree Institution

Variable Label: firstDegreeInstitution

Enter name of institution at which first degree was commenced.

1 Australian National University

2 Barristers Admission Board

3 University of Melbourne

4 University of Queensland

5 University of Sydney

6 University of Western Australia

7 Murdoch University

8 Oxford

\--*End Content for Variable First Degree Institution\--*

69. First Degree Status

Variable Label: firstDegreeStatus

Variable denotes whether the degree was completed or not.

Values:

1 Completed

2 Not completed

\--*End Content for Variable First Degree Status\--*

70. First Degree Year Started

Variable Label: firstDegreeYearStart

Variable denotes the year the degree was commenced.

\--*End Content for Variable First Degree Year Started\--*

71. First Degree Year Completed

Variable Label: firstDegreeYearEnd

Variable denotes the year the degree was completed. If the degree was
not completed code 999.

\--*End Content for Variable First Degree Year Completed\--*

72. Second Degree

Variable Label: secondDegree

See variable First Degree for information.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Degree\--*

73. Second Degree Institution

**Variable Label:** secondDegreeInstitution

See above for information.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Degree Institution\--*

74. Second Degree Status

Variable Label: secondDegreeStatus

Variable denotes whether the degree was completed or not.

Values:

1 Completed

2 Not completed

\--*End Content for Variable Second Degree Status\--*

75. Second Degree Year Started

Variable Label: secondDegreeYearStart

Variable denotes the year the degree was commenced.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Degree Year Started\--*

76. Second Degree Year Completed

Variable Label: secondDegreeYearEnd

Variable denotes the year the degree was completed. If the degree was
not completed code 999.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Degree Year Completed\--*

77. Third Degree

Variable Label: thirdDegree

See above.

\--*End Content for Variable Third Degree\--*

78. Third Degree Institution

**Variable Label:** thirdDegreeInstitution

See above.

\--*End Content for Variable Third Degree Institution\--*

79. Third Degree Status

Variable Label: thirdDegreeStatus

Variable denotes whether the degree was completed or not.

Values:

1 Completed

2 Not completed

\--*End Content for Variable Third Degree Status---*

80. Third Degree Year Started

Variable Label: thirdDegreeYearStart

Variable denotes the year the degree was commenced.

\--*End Content for Variable Third Degree Year Started\--*

81. Third Degree Year Completed

Variable Label: thirdDegreeYearEnd

Variable denotes the year the degree was completed. If the degree was
not completed code 999.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Degree Year Completed\--*

82. Name of Law School

**Variable Label:** nameLawSchool

Replicates above information but provides here for ease of analysis.

\--*End Content for Variable Name of Law School\--*

83. Year of Law School

Variable Label: lastYearLawSchool

Enter year justice graduated from law school.

\--*End Content for Variable Last Year of Law School\--*

84. Academic Honours Medal

Variable Label: academicHonoursMedal

Variable denotes whether justice was awarded the university medal in any
undergraduate degree.

Values:

> 1 Law
>
> 2 Non-law
>
> 3 Law and non-law (multiple)
>
> 4 None recorded

\--*End Content for Variable Academic Honours Medal\--*

85. Academic Honours First Class Honours

Variable Label: academicHonoursFirstClass

Variable denotes whether justice was awarded first class honours in any
undergraduate degree.

Values:

> 1 Law
>
> 2 Other
>
> 3 Multiple
>
> 4 None recorded

\--*End Content for Variable Academic Honours First Class Honours\--*

86. Academic Honours Overseas Scholarship

Variable Label: academicHonoursOverseas

Variable denotes whether justice was awarded a significant scholarship
to study overseas.

Values:

> 1 Rhodes
>
> 2 Fulbright
>
> 3 Other
>
> 4 None recorded

\--*End Content for Variable Academic Honours Overseas\--*

87. Overseas Study

Variable Label: overseasStudy

Binary variable that denotes whether the justice studied outside of
Australia.

Values:

1 Studied overseas

2 Did not study overseas

\--*End Content for Variable Overseas Study\--*

88. Overseas Study Location

Variable Label: overseasStudyLocation

Variable identifies the country in which the justice studied outside of
Australia.

Values:

1 UK

2 USA

3 Other

If not US or UK enter the name of the country.

\--*End Content for Variable Overseas Study\--*

89. First Graduate Degree

Variable Label: firstGradDegree

Enter name of degree.

1 Doctor of Philosophy

2 Master of Laws

3 BCL

4 Other, law

5 Other, non-law

\--*End Content for Variable First Graduate Degree\--*

90. First Graduate Degree Institution

Variable Label: firstGradDegreeInstitution

1 Oxford

2 Cambridge

3 Harvard

4 Yale

5 University of Melbourne

6 University of Sydney

7 Hague

\--*End Content for Variable First Graduate Degree Institution\--*

91. First Graduate Degree Status

Variable Label: firstGradDegreeStatus

Variable denotes whether the degree was completed or not.

Values:

1 Completed

2 Not completed

\--*End Content for Variable First Graduate Degree Status\--*

92. Second Graduate Degree

Variable Label: secondGradDegree

Enter name of degree.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Graduate Degree\--*

93. Second Graduate Degree Institution

Variable Label: secondGradDegreeInstitution

Enter name of institution.

\--*End Content for Variable Second Graduate Degree Institution\--*

94. Second Graduate Degree Status

Variable Label: secondGradDegreeStatus

Variable denotes whether the degree was completed or not.

Values:

1 Completed

2 Not completed

\--*End Content for Variable Second Graduate Degree Status\--*

95. Third Graduate Degree

Variable Label: thirdGradDegree

Enter name of degree.

\--*End Content for Variable Third Graduate Degree\--*

96. Third Graduate Degree Institution

Variable Label: thirdGradDegreeInstitution

Enter name of institution.

\--*End Content for Variable Third Graduate Degree Institution\--*

97. Third Graduate Degree Status

Variable Label: thirdGradDegreeStatus

Variable denotes whether the degree was completed or not.

Values:

1 Completed

2 Not completed

\--*End Content for Variable Third Graduate Degree Status\--*

98. Last Year Attended Graduate Institution

Variable Label: lastYearAttendedGrad

Enter last year nominee attended graduate institution.

\--*End Content for Variable Last Year Attended Graduate Institution\--*

Employment Background Variables

99. Position When Nominated

Variable Label: positionWhenNom

Values:

> 1 Chief Justice of Federal of Australia
>
> 2 Justice of Federal of Australia
>
> 3 Chief Justice of state supreme court/court of appeals
>
> 4 Justice of state supreme court/court of appeals
>
> 5 Commonwealth Solicitor General
>
> 6 State solicitor general
>
> 7 Barrister
>
> 8 Solicitor
>
> 9 Other

\--*End Content for Variable Position When Nominated\--*

100. Position When Nominated Location

Variable Label: positionNomLocation

Location refers to state of registry if federal court justice, and state
of SG/SG if appointment from a state position. Cth SG listed as 9

Values:

> 1 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 2 New South Wales
>
> 3 Northern Territory
>
> 4 Queensland
>
> 5 South Australia
>
> 6 Tasmania
>
> 7 Victoria
>
> 8 Western Australia
>
> 9 Federal (Cth SG only)

\--*End Content for Variable Position When Nominated\--*

101. Nominated from Judiciary

Variable Label: nomFromJudiciary

Denotes whether the nominee was holding judicial office when appointed
(binary).

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

*\--End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service---*

102. Prior Judicial Service

Variable Label: priorJudicialService

Prior judicial appointment (binary). Denotes whether the nominee has
ever served on the judiciary at any point prior to their appointment.

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service\--*

103. Total Months Judicial Service

Variable Label: totalNumMonthsJudicialService

Denotes the total (aggregate) number of months that the nominee has
served on any lower court prior to their HCA appointment. For this
variable and the 4 subsequent variables, any service 15 days and under
in the residual month is rounded down to the closest whole month, and
any service over 15 days in the residual service month is rounded up to
the nearest whole month for the purposes of calculating the total number
of whole months served on the judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Total Months Judicial Service\--*

104. Number of Months State Court Service

Variable Label: numMonthsStateCourtService

Denotes the total number of months that the nominee has served on any
state court prior to their HCA appointment. The variable aggregates any
trial level and appellate level service.

*\--End Content for Variable Number of Months State Court Service\--*

105. Number of Months Federal Court Service

Variable Label: numMonthsFedCourtService

Denotes the total number of months that the nominee has served on any
federal court prior to their HCA appointment. The variable aggregates
any trial level and appellate level service.

*\--End Content for Variable Number of Months Federal Court Service\--*

106. Number of Months Appellate Court Service

Variable Label: numMonthsAppCourtService

Denotes the total number of months that the nominee has served on any
appellate court prior to their HCA appointment. The variable aggregates
federal level and state level service where the court is an appellate
court. For the purpose of this variable and the subsequent variable,
service on the Federal Court of Australia is considered appellate court
service, despite the justices of the Federal Court sitting as trail
judges in some instances.

*\--End Content for Variable Number of Months Appellate Court
Service\--*

107. Number of Months Trial Court Service

Variable Label: numMonthsTrialCourtService

Denotes the total number of months that the nominee has served on any
trial court prior to their HCA appointment. The variable aggregates
federal level and state level service where the court is an trial court.
For the purpose of this variable and the subsequent variable, service on
the Federal Court of Australia is considered appellate court service,
despite the justices of the Federal Court sitting as trail judges in
some instances.

*\--End Content for Variable Number of Months Trial Court Service\--*

108. Prior Judicial Service 1---General

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1General

Where nominee has more than one prior judicial appointment, enter first
in time for this variable.

Values:

> 1 Federal court---trial level \[Any federal court not the FCA or
> Family Court\]
>
> 2 Federal court---appellate level \[Federal Court of Australia\]
>
> 3 Family Court of Australia
>
> 4 State supreme court---trial level
>
> 5 State supreme court---appellate level
>
> 6 State district court (county court)
>
> 7 State local court (magistrates court)
>
> 8 State speciality court

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---General\--*

109. Prior Judicial Service 1---Location

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1Location

Denotes where the justice served.

> **Values:**
>
> 1 Australia (federal actor)
>
> 2 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 3 New South Wales
>
> 4 Northern Territory
>
> 5 Queensland
>
> 6 South Australia
>
> 7 Tasmania
>
> 8 Victoria
>
> 9 Western Australia

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Location\--*

110. Prior Judicial Service 1---Specific

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1Specific

If judicial service was on a federal court, note the registry location
where the justice was based next to the value (e.g. Sydney).

Values:

> 1 Family Court of Australia
>
> 2 Federal Magistrates Court
>
> 3 Federal Circuit Court of Australia
>
> 5 Federal Court of Australia
>
> 6 Australian Industrial Court
>
> 7 Australia Military Court
>
> 8 Commonwealth Court of Conciliation and Arbitration
>
> 9 Commonwealth Industrial Court
>
> 10 Federal Court of Bankruptcy
>
> 11 Industrial Relations Court of Australia
>
> 12 Supreme Court of New South Wales---Common Law Division
>
> 13 Supreme Court of New South Wales---Equity Division
>
> 14 Supreme Court of New South Wales---Court of Appeal
>
> 15 Supreme Court of New South Wales---Court of Criminal Appeal
>
> 16 Land and Environment Court of New South Wales
>
> 17 District Court of New South Wales
>
> 18 Local Court of New South Wales
>
> 19 New South Wales Industrial Court
>
> 20 The Children's Court of New South Wales
>
> 21 New South Wales Dust Diseases Tribunal
>
> 22 Drug Court of New South Wales
>
> 23 New South Wales Chief Industrial Magistrate's Court
>
> 24 New South Wales Coroner's Court
>
> 25 Supreme Court of Queensland---Court of Appeal
>
> 26 Supreme Court of Queensland---Trial Division (Criminal)
>
> 27 Supreme Court of Queensland---Trial Division (Civil)
>
> 28 Industrial Court of Queensland
>
> 29 Queensland Planning and Environment Court
>
> 30 Queensland Land Appeal Court
>
> 31 District Court of Queensland
>
> 32 Magistrates Court of Queensland
>
> 33 Queensland Children's Court
>
> 34 Queensland Mental Health Court
>
> 35 Queensland Murri Court
>
> 36 Queensland Domestic Violence Court
>
> 37 Queensland Drug and Alcohol Court
>
> 38 Queensland Coroner's Court
>
> 39 Supreme Court of Victoria---Court of Appeals
>
> 40 Supreme Court of Victoria---Trial Court Common Law Division
>
> 41 Supreme Court of Victoria---Trial Court Commercial Division
>
> 42 Supreme Court of Victoria---Trial Court Criminal Division
>
> 43 County Court of Victoria
>
> 44 Magistrates' Court of Victoria
>
> 45 Children's Court of Victoria
>
> 46 Victorian Coroner's Court
>
> 47 Supreme Court of South Australia---Trial Division
>
> 48 Supreme Court of South Australia---Criminal Appeal Division
>
> 49 Supreme Court of South Australia---Civil Appeal Division
>
> 50 District Court of South Australia
>
> 51 Magistrates Court of South Australia

52 Environment, Resources, and Development Court of South Australia

> 53 South Australia Industrial Relations Court
>
> 54 Wardens Court of South Australia
>
> 55 Youth Court of South Australia
>
> 56 South Australia Coroner's Court
>
> 57 Supreme Court of Western Australia---Court of Appeals
>
> 58 Supreme Court of Western Australia---General (Trial) Division
>
> 59 Family Court of Western Australia
>
> 60 District Court of Western Australia
>
> 61 Magistrates Court of Western Australia
>
> 62 Aboriginal Community Court of Western Australia
>
> 63 Children's Court of Western Australia
>
> 64 Drug Court of Western Australia
>
> 65 Geraldton Family Violence Court, Western Australia
>
> 66 Western Australia Coroner's Court
>
> 67 Supreme Court of Tasmania---Criminal Division
>
> 68 Supreme Court of Tasmania---Civil Division
>
> 69 Supreme Court of Tasmania---Court of Appeals
>
> 70 Magistrates Court of Tasmania
>
> 71Tasmanian Coroner's Court
>
> 72 Supreme Court of the Northern Territory---Court of Appeal
>
> 73 Supreme Court of the Northern Territory---Court of Criminal Appeal
>
> 74 Supreme Court of the Northern Territory---Civil Trial
>
> 75 Supreme Court of the Northern Territory---Criminal Trial
>
> 76 Northern Territory Local Court (Magistrate's Court)
>
> 77 Coroner's Court of the Northern Territory
>
> 78 Supreme Court of the Australian Capital Territory
>
> 79 Supreme Court of the Australian Capital Territory---Court of Appeal
>
> 80 Magistrates Court of the Australian Capital Territory
>
> 81 Coroner's Court of the Australian Capital Territory
>
> 82 Supreme Court of Norfolk Island
>
> 83 Court of Petty Sessions for Norfolk Island
>
> 84 Nauru Supreme Court
>
> 85 Other

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Specific\--*

111. Prior Judicial Service 1---Start Date

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1StartDate

Enter date first served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Start Date---*

112. Prior Judicial Service 1---End Date

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1EndDate

Enter date last served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---End Date\--*

113. Prior Judicial Service 1---Months Served

Variable Label: numMonthsJudicialService1

Number of years served in months

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Months
Served\--*

114. Prior Judicial Service 1---Year First
     Served

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1FirstYear

Enter year first served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Year First
Served\--*

115. Prior Judicial Service 1---Year Last
     Served

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1LastYear

Enter year last served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Year Last
Served\--*

116. Prior Judicial Service 1---Party
     Appointing

Variable Label: priorJudicialService1Party

Enter the name of the political party who appointed the justice to this
role.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 1---Party\--*

117. Prior Judicial Service 2---General

Variable Label: priorJudicialService2General

Values:

> 1 Federal court---trial level \[Any federal court not the FCA or
> Family Court\]
>
> 2 Federal court---appellate level \[Federal Court of Australia\]
>
> 3 Family Court of Australia
>
> 4 State supreme court---trial level
>
> 5 State supreme court---appellate level
>
> 6 State district court (county court)
>
> 7 State local court (magistrates court)
>
> 8 State speciality court

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 2---General\--*

Variable Label: priorJudicialService2Location

> 6 South Australia

Variable Label: priorJudicialService2StartDate

Enter date first served on judiciary.

role.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 2---Party\--*

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3General

Values:

> 1 Federal court---trial level \[Any federal court not the FCA or
> Family Court\]
>
> 2 Federal court---appellate level \[Federal Court of Australia\]
>
> 3 Family Court of Australia
>
> 4 State supreme court---trial level
>
> 5 State supreme court---appellate level
>
> 6 State district court (county court)
>
> 7 State local court (magistrates court)
>
> 8 State speciality court

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---General\--*

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3Location

> 2 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 3 New South Wales
>
> 4 Northern Territory

> 5 Queensland
>
> 6 South Australia

> 7 Tasmania

> 8 Victoria

> 9 Western Australia

128. Prior Judicial Service 3---Specific

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3Specific

Values:

> 1 Family Court of Australia
>
> 2 Federal Magistrates Court
>
> 3 Federal Circuit Court of Australia
>
> 4 Federal Court of Australia
>
> 6 Australian Industrial Court

129. Prior Judicial Service 3---Start Date

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3StartDate

Enter date first served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---Start Date---*

130. Prior Judicial Service 3---End Date

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3EndDate

Enter date last served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---End Date\--*

131. Prior Judicial Service 3---Months Served

Variable Label: numMonthsJudicialService3

Number of years served in months

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---Months
Served\--*

132. Prior Judicial Service 3---Year First
     Served

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3FirstYear

Enter year first served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---Year First
Served\--*

133. Prior Judicial Service 3---Year Last
     Served

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3LastYear

Enter year last served on judiciary.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---Year Last
Served\--*

134. Prior Judicial Service 3---Party
     Appointing

Variable Label: priorJudicialService3Party

Enter the name of the political party who appointed the justice to this
role.

\--*End Content for Variable Prior Judicial Service 3---Party\--*

135. Admission to the Bar

Variable Label: barAdmission

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable Admission to the Bar\--*

136. Year of First Admission to the Bar

Variable Label: barAdmissionYear

Enter year.

\--*End Content for Variable Year Admitted to the Bar\--*

137. State in Which First Admitted to the Bar

Variable Label: barAdmissionState

Values:

> 1 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 2 New South Wales
>
> 3 Northern Territory
>
> 4 Queensland
>
> 5 South Australia
>
> 6 Tasmania
>
> 7 Victoria
>
> 8 Western Australia
>
> 9 Overseas

\--*End Content for Variable State in Which Admitted to the Bar\--*

138. Promotion to Senior Counsel

Variable Label: barPromotion

1 Yes, SC/QC

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable Promotion to Senior Counsel\--*

139. Year Promoted to Senior Counsel

Variable Label: barPromotionYear

\--*End Content for Variable Year Promoted to Senior Counsel\--*

140. High Court Appearances

Variable Label: hcaAppearances

Variable captures whether the nominee appeared before the High Court as
a barrister.

Values:

1 No

2 Yes---Infrequent \[1-5 appearances\]

3 Yes---Moderate frequency \[6-15 appearances\]

4 Yes---Frequent (more than 15 appearances\]

\--*End Content for Variable High Court Appearances\--*

141. Practice as Solicitor

Variable Label: solicitor

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable Practice as a Solicitor\--*

142. Year First Practiced as Solicitor

Variable Label: solicitorFirstYear

\--*End Content for Variable Year First Practiced as a Solicitor\--*

143. Year Last Practiced as Solicitor

Variable Label: solicitorLastYear

\--*End Content for Variable Year Last Practiced as a Solicitor\--*

144. State Where First Admitted as Solicitor

Variable Label: solicitorState

Values:

> 1 Australian Capital Territory
>
> 2 New South Wales
>
> 3 Northern Territory
>
> 4 Queensland
>
> 5 South Australia
>
> 6 Tasmania
>
> 7 Victoria
>
> 8 Western Australia
>
> 9 Overseas

\--*End Content for Variable State Where Admitted as a Solicitor\--*

145. Law Teaching

Variable Label: teaching

Values:

1 Yes---Fulltime academic

2 Yes---Adjunct/casual academic

3 No

\--*End Content for Variable Law Teaching\--*

146. High Court Associateship

Variable Label: hcaAssociate

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable High Court Associateship\--*

147. Year of High Court Associateship

Variable Label: hcaAssociateYear

\--*End Content for Variable Year of High Court Associateship\--*

148. Justice for High Court Associateship

Variable Label: hcaAssociateJustice

\--*End Content for Variable Justice for High Court Associateship\--*

149. Other Judicial Associateship

Variable Label: otherAssociate

Values:

1 Yes

2 No

\--*End Content for Variable Other Judicial Associateship\--*

150. Year of Other Judicial Associateship

Variable Label: otherAssociateYear

Enter year.

\--*End Content for Variable Year of Other Judicial Associateship\--*

151. Government Appointment Type

Variable Label: govAppointmentType

This variable denotes the type of government appointment previously held
by a justice. I only captured specific appointments, meaning some
government appointments were excluded because they are insufficiently
associated with partisanship. The values denote the appointment types I
included, and I disaggregated between four types of appointment in case
we want to weight them differently.

Values:

1 Commonwealth Solicitor-General

2 State Solicitor-General

3 Commonwealth tribunal appointment (e.g., AAT)

> 4 Commonwealth commission (major commission only, e.g., Human Rights
> Commission, Law Reform Commission)

999 Not applicable (i.e., no relevant prior government position held by
the justice)

\--*End Content for Variable Government Appointment Type\--*

152. Government Appointment Party

Variable Label: governmentAppointmentParty

Denotes the political party who appointed the justice to this role.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Government Appointment Party\--*

153. Political Party Commonwealth

Variable Label: politicalPartyCth

Variable denotes whether the justice has political experience in federal
politics, standing or holding office as a member of parliament.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Political Party Commonwealth\--*

154. Known Political Identification

Variable Label: politicalIdentification

Variable denotes whether the justice is known to have membership in, or
overtly express support for a specific political party. Includes youth
party groups at university.

Values:

1 Liberal

2 Labor

999 Not applicable

\--*End Content for Variable Known Political Identification---*

155. Notes

Variable Label: Notes

Any notes to the data.

\--*End Content for Variable Notes\--*


## Citation

If you use this data, please cite:

Leslie, P., Robinson, Z., Smyth, R., & Jacobi, T. (2026). Supporting comparative studies of
judicial behavior: Introducing the Australian High Court Database. *Journal of Law & Empirical
Analysis*, *3*(1), 251--263. <https://doi.org/10.1177/2755323X261438254>

```bibtex

  author  = {Pat Leslie and Zo\"{e} Robinson and Russell Smyth and Tonja Jacobi},
  title   = {Supporting Comparative Studies of Judicial Behavior: Introducing the Australian High Court Database},
```
